name: Authentic New England Fish Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 lb frozen haddock (or cod fillets) not breaded
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the salt pork into 1-inch pieces and place in a kettle.
Simmer slowly until fat is extracted.
2. Add sliced onion and sauté until wilted.
3. Add diced potatoes and water.
4. Place frozen fish on top of the mixture in the kettle.
5. Cook slowly, breaking up the fish with a fork as it thaws.
6. If necessary, add more water to keep the consistency minimal.
7. Cook until potatoes are soft and slightly mushy.
8. Add milk and seasoning, then bring almost to a boil.
9. Stir in butter and serve.
(Clam Chowder: Use 2 cans of Snows Eastern clam pieces instead of water. Add clams after potatoes are well cooked, then add milk as before. One can of clam pieces and one can of Snows clams may be used. Snows clams and pieces make an excellent substitute for fresh eastern clams. Serves 4.)
